label: Possessives
description: mio, tuo, suo and the rest: the possessive plus its article, agreeing with the thing owned, and the family-noun exception.

label: Possessive + article, agreeing
description: The possessive takes the definite article and agrees with the thing owned, not the owner.

attributes.canonical_example: il mio libro, la mia casa, i miei amici, le sue sorelle
attributes.common_miss: mio libro (no article); il mio casa (agreement); suo for the owner's gender

label: Family nouns drop the article
description: A singular, unmodified family noun takes no article; the article returns in the plural or when the noun is modified.

attributes.canonical_example: mia madre, mio fratello; ma la mia mamma, i miei fratelli
attributes.common_miss: la mia madre; il mio fratello

label: Possessive pronouns
description: The possessive used on its own (with the article) to mean mine, yours, and so on.

attributes.canonical_example: Questo e il mio; Il tuo dov'e?
attributes.common_miss: dropping the article on the standalone possessive

label: suo: his, her, your (formal)
description: suo agrees with the thing owned, so context resolves whether it is his, her or your (formal).

label: suo/sua: his, her, your (formal)
description: The third-person possessive suo agrees with the thing owned, not the owner, so context alone decides whether it means his, her, its or your (formal, Lei). Comprehension items resolve the owner from context; production items check that the learner reaches for suo regardless of the owner's gender.

attributes.canonical_example: il suo cane (his or her dog); i Suoi documenti (your documents, formal)
attributes.common_miss: sua for a male owner / suo for a female owner (agreeing with the owner instead of the noun)

label: Predicate: è mio
description: Ownership after essere drops the article: Di chi è? È mio. Questa borsa è mia. Distinct from the article-bearing pronoun (la mia è più grande).

attributes.canonical_example: Di chi è questa borsa? È mia.
attributes.common_miss: È la mia for plain ownership (article intrudes).

label: Postposed: casa mia, colpa mia
description: The possessive after the noun in the productive fixed patterns: casa mia/tua/sua, colpa mia, piacere mio, Dio mio. Word order is the skill; the fully lexicalised extremes are vocabulary.

attributes.common_miss: a mia casa (calqued order); alla casa mia.

label: Proprio
description: The reflexive possessive: with impersonal and third-person subjects (Ognuno pensa ai propri interessi), and as the disambiguator where suo is ambiguous.

attributes.common_miss: suo where proprio is required with impersonal subjects.
